action_items.item: Write the instructions for the warehouse adapters (adapter-specific install/inspection guidance).

bugs.summary: Status roll-up inconsistency across Zone → Aisle → Section → Rail
bugs.detail: Status is computed independently per level instead of rolled up, so a parent can show trouble while a child shows good (and vice versa). Breaks user trust: false negatives hide real problems; false positives train people to ignore warnings.
bugs.defined_logic: 'In Progress' is a GATE, not a health verdict — a parent stays In Progress until every descendant is installed AND all Test-Pending are cleared; only then does it resolve to Complete (all pass) or Trouble (any Has-Issues, or any Flag remaining). Flagged installation blockers hold In Progress until everything else clears, then escalate to Trouble.

projects.notes: TOP-PRIORITY BUG (fix by Fri 7/3, owned by dev — Eric/Cristina, gate-vs-display TBD; Clay flagging only): status roll-up inconsistency across Zone → Aisle → Section → Rail — status computed independently per level instead of rolled up, so a parent can show trouble while a child shows good and vice versa; breaks user trust (false negatives hide real problems, false positives train people to ignore warnings). DEFINED LOGIC: 'In Progress' is a GATE, not a health verdict — a parent stays In Progress until every descendant is installed AND all Test-Pending are cleared, then resolves to Complete (all pass) or Trouble (any Has-Issues, or any Flag remaining); flagged installation blockers hold In Progress until everything else clears, then escalate to Trouble. DEPLOY WALL confirmed Fri 7/3 — all features anchored; Walmart approval gates the deploy column. Feature-stage grid (Design→FE→BE→Test→Deploy): Installation (WMPC) fully complete, all five stages green; Sections Build FE reopened to absorb the 1-Foot Modular (resolved with Jeremy, now a design input — no longer a standalone item), deploy 7/3; Add Torch (registering flow) design done, FE not started, deploy 7/3; Escape Button (registering page) design done, needs UI / FE not started, deploy 7/3. First Users MS Account complete (contractor access provisioning chain). Open action item: Clay to write the warehouse-adapter instructions (adapter-specific install/inspection guidance). Validation = supervisor-facing labor-state board (not-yet-installed → installed-not-tested → tested-not-passed → passed) producing the digital QC record; Zel does the physical inspection. Maintenance UI delivered/done (Clay's part, has user feedback); remaining monitoring & alerting is net-new backend, scoped separately, real deadline tracks rail deployment rather than 7/10. Weekly deck (TSC_Weekly) rebuilt to 6/24: Slide 2 (Feature Stages) regenerated via build_slide2.js with a priority-callout banner + 6-row grid; Slide 3 MS Account cell grey → green; title date 6/17 → 6/24. val_aisle.html — Run Test: removed the fake 2.4s test simulation. Tap now sets per-rail testsRunning_ state (session-persisted) and grays the button to 'Test Running' (disabled, clock icon); card status/color/pills unchanged (last-known data). Refresh re-renders gray while in flight. New top-right slide-in toast ('Test started — up to 20 min / runs in background'), stacks and auto-dismisses. projects.name: QC Tool — Post-Install Inspection
projects.serves: TopStock Rail / Walmart first; multi-product later
supporting: Eric (backend — data pull reuses his pipeline)
projects.notes: Post-install inspection, TopStock/Walmart priority. v1 (Working, 7/10) = one UI (designed to hold images) + data pull reusing Eric's pipeline + text deficiencies + hosted + internal-only RBAC. Complete-with-images ~7/25 is an estimate, gated on the image-storage decision; images are a fast-follow staged into the same UI. Build happens in its own project; underway against mock store 100. Full audit loop works end-to-end (adapter picker → scanner → 3-step questions → save → home); completed rails persist to IndexedDB and home progress/zone/adapter counts advance live. audit_questions.html = 3 steps (adapter checks varying by type, shared rail checks, ESL three-way Correct/Wrong Location/No Image). Scanner: manual entry auto-formats to XXXX-XXXX with Continue gated on a valid 8-char ID; duplicate-rail scans warn-and-overwrite (latest wins per session); location-confirm 'No' keeps the adapter choice; confirm-location modal no longer opens on page load. Sampling locked: ±7% margin = 167 rails for store 100, proportional across 8 letter-zones (zone = leading alpha of the section code), adapter targets 85% standard / 10% warehouse / 5% wire frame, fill-as-you-go. View-Data/PC dashboard built (data_dashboard.html): columnar cross-filter view — 3 defect donuts (all/adapter/rail), per-adapter breakdown cards, filter-aware blue completion bar, store/zone/adapter filters, store-aware CSV export; reads all audit records across sessions (getAllAuditRecords), store-stamped saves (untagged legacy → Store 100), in-memory demo mode (Demo Store 1/2/3, never persisted, off by default, amber toggle + banner); nav reordered so activity precedes store pick (login→flow_pick, audit→store→audit_home, view→dashboard), auto-save-per-rail replaces the old Save-session button. Open gaps tracked as carry-forwards: derived_severity rules undefined, Warehouse/Wire Frame wording provisional, third adapter unreleased, Azure-vs-Supabase backend decision pending the Azure tenant answer. Outcome = a reliable post-install QC record for TopStock.

changelog.entry: Tracker initialized. Committed 7/10 delivery.

v1 (Working) target set 7/15 (was 7/10). Scope defined: single image-ready UI + Eric-pipeline data pull + text deficiencies + hosted + internal-only RBAC. Complete-with-images ~7/25 fast-follow, gated on image-storage decision. Starts after the 6/22 test install.
Data model v0.1 drafted and finalized as the build contract — entities, conditional per-adapter inspection items, frozen+versioned sample plan; date unchanged.
Store-100 ingest parser built — expands 8-ft sections into L/R rails (1,081 rails across 8 letter-zones) as the mock dataset behind dataService.js.
Sample size set at ±7% margin = 167 rails, proportional by zone, with adapter targets (85% standard / 10% warehouse / 5% wire frame) as fill-as-you-go.

Frontend build started against mock data: login, store selector, flow picker, audit home (sampling hub), adapter picker (soft quota warning), camera scanner (BarcodeDetector + ZXing fallback, Scandit-ready) with morph-to-button and confirm-location modal. Status → in_progress.

Inspection-checklist blocker cleared for Standard adapter (rivet/flat/tight checks confirmed verbally + via user guide); Warehouse + Wire Frame wording still provisional pending team/engineering, third adapter unreleased — narrowed to carry-forward cf-qc-inspection-checklist.

Full audit loop working end-to-end against mock store 100 (adapter picker → scanner → 3-step questions → save → home); completed rails write to IndexedDB and home progress/zone/adapter counts advance live.
Built audit_questions.html (3 steps: adapter checks varying by type, shared rail checks, ESL three-way Correct/Wrong Location/No Image) and wired the save-session flow.

Scanner fixes: manual entry auto-formats to XXXX-XXXX with Continue gated on a valid 8-char ID; duplicate-rail scans warn-and-overwrite (latest wins per session); location-confirm 'No' keeps the adapter choice; confirm-location modal no longer opens on page load.
Sampling locked at ±7% margin = 167 rails for store 100, proportional across 8 letter-zones (zone = leading alpha of the section code), adapter targets 85/10/5 as fill-as-you-go.
Date reconciled: v1 (Working) due corrected 7/15 → 7/10 to match Clay's restated commitment (7/10 unchanged); Complete-with-images ~7/25 estimate unchanged.

Logged open QC carry-forwards (flagged to carry weekly): derived_severity rules undefined (cf-qc-derived-severity), View Data dashboard not built (cf-qc-view-data), Azure-vs-Supabase backend decision pending Azure tenant answer (cf-qc-backend-decision).

Removed all visible 'provisional' tags from the adapter picker and question screens (flag retained in data only); audit flow now reads as final to the auditor. Underlying Warehouse/Wire Frame wording remains provisional per cf-qc-inspection-checklist.

Built data_dashboard.html (View-Data/PC side): columnar dashboard — 3 cross-filtered defect donuts (all/adapter/rail), per-adapter breakdown cards, filter-aware blue completion bar, store/zone/adapter filters, store-aware CSV export.
Added getAllAuditRecords() to dataService.js — dashboard reads all records across sessions (viewing is session-less); fixed null-session IDBKeyRange crash with a guard.
Added store-stamping to saveAuditRecord — records now filter by store; untagged legacy records default to Store 100.
Added in-memory demo mode to the dashboard (Demo Store 1/2/3, never persisted, starts OFF) with amber toggle + warning banner.

Reordered nav so activity precedes store pick (login→flow_pick; audit→store picker→audit_home; view→dashboard direct); removed 'Save session' from audit_home in favor of auto-save-per-rail + 'View data' exit.
Carry-forward cf-qc-view-data RESOLVED — View-Data dashboard built (data_dashboard.html).

projects.name: Site Survey Tool
projects.next_milestone: Prove ARKit / capture spine — go/no-go checkpoint (gates the internal-build-vs-buy thesis)

environment.primary_device: Personal iPhone 15 Pro (LiDAR-equipped) — the dev/test device, via Xcode sideload
environment.signing: Free Apple ID — 7-day provisioning profile; re-run from Xcode to renew
environment.setup_gauntlet: Cleared: signing, Developer Mode, device trust, camera permission, iOS 17 deployment target
environment.distribution_trigger: $99/yr Apple Developer account + TestFlight, once the tool is validated, to distribute to additional testers
environment.capture_rig: Clamp mount on the cart child-seat bar; phone at ~45° shooting left-side shelving. Mount angle = a fixed design parameter for the planned cart holder. Confirmed load-bearing, not optional — the measurement operating envelope depends on it.

validation.tag_count: 101 detected vs 104 actual on real fixtures (97%)
validation.section_detection: 3 sections found for 3 walked; shelf-level detection functioning
validation.length_within_session: Tight within a session (~1–2 in spread)
validation.length_cross_session: Varies with standoff distance and camera angle — same setup gave 8-inch session-to-session swings when the mount drifted off 45°
validation.method_comparison: Raycast method drift-stable (shrugged off a 31.6-inch path deviation); camera-travel method inflated under drift. Built a shelf-derived aisle axis (from shelf-plane facing, not the walked path) so both length measures project onto a drift-immune reference — pending morning straight-vs-drift walk comparison.
validation.key_insight: 'Rigid breaks, flexible works' — measurement must tolerate cart/angle wobble, not assume a fixed 45°.
validation.operating_envelope: Min standoff bounded by camera FOV (can't capture an 81-inch top shelf below ~42 inches at 45°); max bounded by precision degrading with distance. Fixed-angle production cart mount is load-bearing.
validation.next_problem: Shelf-edge definition (landing dots on the lip where tags live) is the next major problem after length.

projects.notes: ARKit is the foundation of the internal build (scene-reconstruction mesh + LiDAR, NOT RoomPlan — its furniture taxonomy and ~2,000 sq ft single-scan ceiling don't fit retail fixtures at store scale; we own the interpretation step). Polycam / Matterport are build-vs-buy ALTERNATIVES, not a fallback — if ARKit can't prove out, escalate the build-vs-buy decision. ARKit testing is planned work, not a threat, so the project is in_progress (not at-risk). Hardware dependency accepted: usable LiDAR + ARKit only on iPhone Pro / iPad Pro (no standard iPhone, no Android) — per-device cost trivial vs solution-architect time. Direction deck (7 slides, RF Slate) + working v0.0.01 demo presented to the technical group, well received; key challenge = prove the tool measures fixtures to the accuracy we need (±0.5 inch target, error bands, honest pass/fail across shelf bay width, shelf height, gondola length/depth, aisle width; tiered T0 methodology → T1 controlled accuracy → T2 real store aisle → T3 derived counts). Built AisleMeasure — a native ARKit + LiDAR app with (a) a tap-to-measure validation mode (labeled measurements, CSV error-table export vs the ±0.5 inch target) and (b) a walk-the-aisle auto-capture mode (Vision-based ESL tag counting with 3D dedup, LiDAR-mesh shelf-level detection, per-section width/shelf/tag breakdown, drift estimate, CSV export). Section segmentation (bay-boundary detection) is the first iteration area — v1 infers boundaries from tag-density gaps and under-segments butted bays (tracked as cf-sitesurvey-segmentation). Paired with an expectation-vs-reality validation workbook mirroring both CSV exports (pre-walk thresholds, auto-computed error + pass/fail, per-failure-mode cause tracking: Vision / mesh / segmentation / tracking). AisleMeasure is building AND running on the iPhone 15 Pro (LiDAR-equipped, iOS 26.4) via free Apple ID signing against the Xcode iOS 26.5 SDK — the free-ID profile lasts 7 days, so it's re-run from Xcode to renew; fine for dev/test on the device on hand. Distribution path: a $99/yr Apple Developer account + TestFlight is the flagged trigger for pushing the build to additional testers, pulled once the tool is validated (not before). Dev/test runs on Clay's personal iPhone 15 Pro via Xcode sideload — the work phone is excluded (likely MDM-managed). Capture validation (real fixtures): the LiDAR mesh captures dense, clean geometry at cart working distance, and the mesh depth-color gradient cleanly separates the near target run from the far aisle. Added orientation gating — the app learns the run's facing direction from vertical shelf-panel mesh normals and rejects perpendicular cross-aisle geometry that depth gating alone can't exclude; built with a discard/file-separately switch so the same partition logic can feed future whole-store stitching. Capture rig defined: clamp mount on the cart child-seat bar, phone at ~45° shooting left-side shelving (mount angle is a fixed design parameter for the planned cart holder, to remove camera-orientation uncertainty). Setup gauntlet cleared (signing, Developer Mode, device trust, camera permission, iOS 17 deployment target). Core detectors validated on real fixtures: tag count 101 vs 104 actual (97%), shelf-level + section detection working (3 sections found for 3 walked). Breakthrough: the near-plane gate was REMOVED entirely — it was rejecting up to 100% of detections on wrong assumptions about the diagonal cart-mount geometry, the root saboteur on both the tag and shelf pipelines. All detection moved off the main thread (fixed the camera freeze); tag positions now come from LiDAR depth unprojection rather than thread-unsafe ARView raycast. Debug overlay added (live detection boxes, shelf-candidate dots, per-stage diagnostic counters) plus a gating on/off toggle — this instrumentation is what let every failure be diagnosed by sight instead of guessed at. UI: length shown in feet-and-inches; summarize step backgrounded with a working spinner; total shelf count made cumulative across sections (BOM-relevant) rather than distinct heights; world-space boundary bar (raycast at a fixed left-third line) lets the operator mark a deliberate start/stop span to measure against a tape; side still hardcoded LEFT pending real UI. Length findings: within-session readings are tight (~1–2 in) but accuracy varies with standoff distance and camera angle (8-inch session-to-session swings when the mount drifted off 45°). KEY INSIGHT — 'rigid breaks, flexible works': measurement must tolerate cart/angle wobble, not assume a fixed 45°. The raycast method proved drift-stable (shrugged off a 31.6-inch path deviation) while camera-travel inflated under drift; built a shelf-derived aisle axis (from shelf-plane facing, not the walked path, so it's drift-immune) onto which both length measures project — pending a morning straight-vs-drift walk comparison. Operating envelope: min standoff bounded by camera FOV (can't capture an 81-inch top shelf below ~42 inches at 45°), max by precision degrading with distance — so a fixed-angle production cart mount is load-bearing, not optional. Shelf-edge definition (landing dots on the lip where tags live) is flagged as the next major problem after length. Open carry-forwards: tag detector counts any tag-shaped rectangle (barcode/signage false-positives), deferred to a detection-refinement pass (cf-sitesurvey-tag-detector); section segmentation still inferred from tag-density gaps — works on clean runs, untested on butted bays (cf-sitesurvey-segmentation); a ~13-inch systematic short-read offset on straight walks remains uncharacterized (cf-sitesurvey-length-offset). (MeshWalk was the earlier minimal mesh-viewer prototype.) Built a standalone timeline drill-down (matches the QC/GT Gantt format); new deck element: orange diamond = go/no-go checkpoint. Capture-screen animation fixed (walk-to-dot rides the perspective line to the aisle vanishing point; perspective easing; DOT_START/DOT_PERSP tunable). Phased 6-week runway: general design DONE (wk of 6/15); Prove ARKit / capture spine → go/no-go checkpoint ~7/3 (the highest-leverage decision — gates both downstream phases and the entire internal-build-vs-buy thesis); Data + RF ~6/29–7/15; align derived outputs with the broader team ~7/13–7/22; in-store demo committed wk of 7/27. Build sequence: 1 Capture spine → 2 Data + RF → 3 Derived outputs → 4 Demo store; backlog tracked separately.

roadmap.name: Capture spine
roadmap.detail: ARKit scene-reconstruction mesh, cart-mount pose stability, frame + dot-stitch loop

roadmap.name: Data + RF
roadmap.detail: observation/interpretation schema, BLE + WiFi sampling on the pose, checkpoint + confidence pass

roadmap.name: Derived outputs
roadmap.detail: fixture geometry → counts, semantic aisle labeling v1, Studio Evo Maps export path

roadmap.name: Demo store
roadmap.detail: end-to-end run in one store, ground-truth error check, polish capture UX

backlog: Semantic-OCR aisle-label normalization across languages and retailer vernaculars
Graceful degradation for non-grid layouts, multi-level stores, blocked aisles, reflective freezer runs, resumable sessions
Anchor-tag placement left deliberately undefined — to be settled empirically by the tool's own RF data

phases.name: Prove ARKit / capture spine
phases.note: highest-leverage decision; gates both downstream phases and the internal-build-vs-buy thesis

phases.name: Align derived outputs with broader team

changelog.entry: Tracker initialized. In planning; no committed dates yet.

Capture-screen walk-to-dot animation fixed — dot now rides the perspective line from the centered reticle out to the aisle's vanishing point (off-screen right, near eye level), no longer floating on the wrong shelf.

Per-dot drift corrected — added perspective easing (dot hangs back when far, rushes in on arrival like a fixed world point), shortened per-dot travel, re-anchored stops to their landing frame so the last dot lands in-frame; DOT_START and DOT_PERSP exposed as tunable constants.

Rebuilt the 7-slide direction deck in the RF Slate palette; reframed the capture flow from concept to working v0.0.01; added phone-screenshot drop slots on the capture slide for device mockups; built to stand alone if distributed.

Capture framework settled — build directly on ARKit scene-reconstruction mesh + LiDAR, NOT RoomPlan (RoomPlan's furniture taxonomy and ~2,000 sq ft single-scan ceiling don't fit retail fixtures at store scale; we want to own the interpretation step).

Hardware dependency confirmed — usable LiDAR + ARKit only on iPhone Pro / iPad Pro (no standard iPhone, no Android); stated as a deliberate, accepted tradeoff (per-device cost trivial vs solution-architect time).
Presented the direction deck + v0.0.01 demo to the technical group; both well received; key challenge raised = proving the tool actually measures the fixtures we need to the accuracy we need.

Committed to a hands-on ARKit LiDAR capture test (iPhone 16 Pro + M4 MacBook Air); built a minimal mesh-viewer app (MeshWalk) to walk an aisle, visualize the scene mesh, and spot-check measurements against a tape measure.

BLOCKER: work MacBook locked, gating the MeshWalk build; chasing IT unlock + Xcode install + personal-Apple-ID signing on one ticket. Polycam + Apple Measure staged on the phone as a no-Mac fallback (same ARKit + LiDAR stack).

Defined the measurement-validation methodology: ground truth (tape) vs tool across fixture dimensions (shelf bay width, shelf height, gondola length/depth, aisle width), reported as error bands against a ±0.5 inch target, with honest pass/fail rather than a polished claim.

Validation structured in tiers: Tier 0 methodology (no dependencies), Tier 1 dimensional accuracy on a controlled/fallback capture, Tier 2 real store aisle, Tier 3 derived counts (facings, adaptors) if dimensions pass.

Day-by-day plan to a Wed-AM presentation: Wed clear blockers (IT ticket + install fallback apps), Thu build MeshWalk or fall back to Polycam, Fri first real store aisle capture + ground-truth sheet (critical day), Mon analyze into an error table + optional second pass, Tue build the validation deck with real data.
DATE: measurement-proof / validation results due Tuesday night 6/23, presenting first thing Wednesday morning 6/24 (first committed Site Survey date).

RISK: the plan has one fragile point — store capture must happen Friday 6/19; if the Mac unlock slips, capture proceeds Friday with Polycam regardless, because the validation question is about the sensor, not our code.

ROADMAP build sequence set (4 steps): 1) Capture spine — ARKit mesh, cart-mount pose stability, frame+dot-stitch loop; 2) Data + RF — observation/interpretation schema, BLE+WiFi sampling on the pose, checkpoint+confidence pass; 3) Derived outputs — fixture geometry→counts, semantic aisle labeling v1, Studio Evo Maps export path; 4) Demo store — end-to-end run in one store, ground-truth error check, polish capture UX.

BACKLOG/future logged: semantic-OCR aisle-label normalization across languages/vernaculars; retailer privacy + interior-imagery handling; passive cart-mount physical design (possibly 3D-printable); graceful degradation (non-grid layouts, multi-level, blocked aisles, reflective freezer runs, resumable sessions); anchor-tag placement left deliberately undefined, to be settled empirically by the tool's own RF data.

Build-vs-buy settled: build on raw ARKit, not an existing scanner engine (e.g. Polycam). Existing scanners are export-only / not embeddable — routing capture through one would impose their UX + interpretation on a retail-survey job, lose the single-walk geometry+RF capture, and make a consumer app's pricing/format/existence a load-bearing dependency. Same Apple sensor + pose either way; interpretation and capture UX are the actual product and stay in-house. Polycam remains only a no-Mac validation fallback.
Built AisleMeasure — a native ARKit + LiDAR tap-to-measure validation app for fixture dimensional-accuracy testing against the ±0.5 inch target, with labeled measurements and CSV error-table export.

Extended AisleMeasure with a walk-the-aisle auto-capture mode: Vision-based ESL tag counting with 3D dedup, LiDAR-mesh shelf-level detection, per-section width/shelf/tag breakdown, a drift estimate, and CSV export.

Flagged section segmentation (bay-boundary detection) as the first area needing iteration — v1 infers boundaries from tag-density gaps and will under-segment butted bays. Logged as carry-forward cf-sitesurvey-segmentation.

Built an expectation-vs-reality validation workbook mirroring both AisleMeasure CSV exports — pre-walk conditions/thresholds set before the walk, auto-computed error and pass/fail, and per-failure-mode cause tracking (Vision / mesh / segmentation / tracking).

Delivery: AisleMeasure packaged as a sideloadable Xcode project with a free-Apple-ID signing guide; now awaiting iPhone 16 Pro arrival, then an Xcode build (expect compile errors to clear) before the first store walk. Blocker reframed from the locked MacBook to device arrival + build; Polycam fallback retained.

Status corrected at_risk → in_progress — ARKit testing is planned work, not a threat. Removed the at-risk framing and the Polycam-fallback language from the overview; device-arrival → Xcode-build is now a near-term gate in notes, not a blocker.

Reframed Polycam/Matterport as build-vs-buy ALTERNATIVES, not a fallback. ARKit is the foundation of the internal build; if ARKit can't prove out → escalate the build-vs-buy decision. Supersedes the earlier 'Polycam validation fallback' framing.

Timeline restructured into phases (6-week runway): general design DONE (wk 6/15); Prove ARKit/capture spine → go/no-go ~7/3 (gates internal-build thesis); Data+RF ~6/29–7/15; align derived outputs w/ broader team ~7/13–7/22; in-store demo committed wk of 7/27. next_milestone reanchored from the 6/23 measurement-proof to the ~7/3 ARKit go/no-go.
Built a standalone timeline drill-down (matches the QC/GT Gantt format); new deck element: orange diamond = go/no-go checkpoint.
ARKit go/no-go ~7/3 flagged as the highest-leverage decision point — gates both downstream phases and the entire internal-build-vs-buy thesis.

Dev/test environment set: primary device = iPhone 15 Pro (LiDAR-equipped) on iOS 26.4, building against the Xcode iOS 26.5 SDK. Settles the device question — build proceeds on the iPhone 15 Pro on hand; supersedes the earlier 'awaiting iPhone 16 Pro arrival' note.

AisleMeasure now building AND running on the iPhone 15 Pro via free Apple ID signing (7-day provisioning profile; re-run from Xcode to renew). $99/yr Apple Developer account + TestFlight flagged as the trigger to distribute to additional testers, pulled once the tool is validated.

Known issue logged: the tag detector currently counts any tag-shaped rectangle, so it will false-positive on barcodes, shelf-talkers, and signage. Deferred to a detection-refinement pass after the core framework (plane gating, segmentation, walk-and-count) is validated. Tracked as carry-forward cf-sitesurvey-tag-detector.
Device clarified: AisleMeasure runs on Clay's personal iPhone 15 Pro (LiDAR) via Xcode sideload — the personal phone is the dev/test device; the work phone is excluded (likely MDM-managed).

Capture validation: confirmed the LiDAR mesh captures dense, clean geometry of real retail fixtures at cart working distance; the mesh depth-color gradient cleanly separates the near target run from the far aisle.

Added orientation gating: the app learns the run's facing direction from vertical shelf-panel mesh normals and rejects perpendicular cross-aisle geometry that depth gating alone can't exclude. Built with a discard/file-separately switch so the same partition logic can feed future whole-store stitching.

Capture rig defined: clamp mount on the cart child-seat bar, phone at ~45° shooting left-side shelving; mount angle flagged as a fixed design parameter for the planned cart holder, to remove camera-orientation uncertainty.

Engineering: automated the near-plane band (removed the manual slider) and moved tag/shelf detection off the main thread to fix a camera freeze; capture screen decluttered, side hardcoded LEFT pending real UI.

Carry-forward reaffirmed: tag detector still counts any tag-shaped rectangle (barcode/signage false-positives) — remains open as cf-sitesurvey-tag-detector, deferred to the detection-refinement pass after core-framework validation.
AisleMeasure builds and runs on the iPhone 15 Pro via Xcode sideload — full setup gauntlet cleared (signing, Developer Mode, device trust, camera permission, iOS 17 deployment target).
Core detectors validated on real fixtures: tag count 101 vs 104 actual (97%); shelf-level and section detection functioning (3 sections found for 3 walked).

Removed the near-plane gate entirely — it was rejecting up to 100% of detections on wrong assumptions about the diagonal cart-mount geometry, and was the root saboteur on both the tag and shelf pipelines.
Refined orientation gating to reject perpendicular cross-aisle geometry past the aisle end, keeping the discard/file-separately switch reserved for future whole-store stitching.
Moved all detection off the main thread (fixed the camera freeze); tag positions now derive from LiDAR depth unprojection instead of thread-unsafe ARView raycast.

Added a debug overlay (live detection boxes, shelf-candidate dots, per-stage diagnostic counters) and a gating on/off toggle — the instrumentation that let every failure be diagnosed by sight rather than guessed at.
UI: switched length display to feet-and-inches; backgrounded the summarize step with a working spinner; made total shelf count cumulative across sections (BOM-relevant) rather than distinct heights.
Built a world-space boundary bar (raycast at a fixed left-third line) so the operator can mark a deliberate start/stop span and measure it against a tape.

Measurement validation findings: tag detection still over-counts (fires on any rectangle, not just ESL tags) — deferred to the detection-refinement pass; shelf-edge definition (landing dots on the lip where tags live) flagged as the next major problem after length.

KEY INSIGHT — 'rigid breaks, flexible works': measurement must tolerate cart/angle wobble, not assume a fixed 45°. Length is highly angle-sensitive — the same setup gave 8-inch session-to-session swings when the mount drifted off 45°.

Length validation: within-session readings are tight (~1–2 in spread), but accuracy varies with standoff distance and camera angle. The raycast method proved drift-stable (shrugged off a 31.6-inch path deviation) while the camera-travel method inflated under drift.

Built a shelf-derived aisle axis (drift-immune, taken from shelf-plane facing rather than the walked path) so both length measures project onto a stable reference; pending morning validation with a straight-vs-drift walk comparison.

Operating-envelope thinking: min standoff is bounded by camera FOV (can't capture an 81-inch top shelf below ~42 inches at 45°), max by precision degrading with distance — fixed-angle production cart mount confirmed as load-bearing, not optional.

New carry-forward cf-sitesurvey-length-offset: a ~13-inch systematic short-read offset on straight walks remains uncharacterized. Reaffirmed open: tag-detector barcode/signage false-positives (cf-sitesurvey-tag-detector) and tag-density-gap section segmentation untested on butted bays (cf-sitesurvey-segmentation).

carry_forwards.rule: Carries forward weekly until resolved. Cannot be deleted — only resolved with date + resolution note.

carry_forwards.item: Site Survey AisleMeasure — a ~13-inch systematic short-read offset on straight walks is still uncharacterized. Within-session length readings are tight (~1–2 in spread), but a consistent short-read bias remains; root cause not yet isolated.
carry_forwards.rule: Carries forward weekly until resolved. Cannot be deleted — only resolved with date + resolution note.

recurring_deliverables.name: Weekly leadership slide
recurring_deliverables.standard: Thaddeus directive: single slide, max 200 words, delete unused boxes, relevance over completeness

recurring_deliverables.name: Weekly recap email
recurring_deliverables.standard: Subject format: Weekly Recap | [Date] | Product Implementation. SharePoint links left as [LINK] placeholders.

operating_principles: Deployment-to-results: every performance result tied to a specific deployment config (this config = this performance = this cost)
Unresolved gaps carry forward every week without exception, flagged until nauseating
Track timelines by owner, commitment, and outcome — not by who is nominally in charge

portfolio_risks.risk: Eric's data pipeline plus his post-6/22 bandwidth is the shared dependency across Companion Validation, QC Tool data pull, and Maintenance monitoring/alerting.
portfolio_risks.impact: Single biggest portfolio risk — manage Eric's queue deliberately.
